mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls captures aggregate wage income dynamics; sustained positive employment growth drives household consumption and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or top-quintile PAYEMS growth months do not show higher positive-return frequency than bottom-quintile months (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates the equity discount rate and tightens financial conditions sufficiently to overwhelm earnings growth expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the mean 60-day forward return conditional on the trigger is not significantly below zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in industrial production and rising unemployment creates a coincident profit squeeze and demand shock that single-factor models fail to capture, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO monthly change and UNRATE monthly change adds no incremental explanatory power (incremental R-squared < 0.01) in a 3-year rolling regression predicting 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month CPI acceleration forces equity markets to revise terminal rate expectations upward, compressing valuation multiples and generating negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, 5-day forward returns following CPIAUCSL acceleration triggers are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1.5% signals a sudden consumer demand retrenchment that leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as analysts cut revenue forecasts.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of occurrences, or the mean 5-day forward return conditional on the trigger is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales captures the velocity of consumer spending; higher sales growth translates into top-line revenue surprises for consumer-facing corporations and the broader S&P 500, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between RSAFS monthly change and 20-day forward returns is not positive and statistically significant (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window, or top quintile fails to outperform bottom quintile.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, emerging labor market slack triggers negative earnings revision momentum and risk premium expansion, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes fail to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mean 60-day forward return conditional on the trigger is not below zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts momentum leads construction activity and durable goods demand through employment and material orders; positive month-over-month growth in housing starts generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days via cyclical sector earnings leverage.
failure_condition: Positive HOUST momentum decile does not outperform negative momentum decile over any 3-year rolling window (mean return spread < 0), or rank correlation is below 0.03.
